channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: DNS TTL Best Practices: How to Choose the Right Values
item.description: Every DNS record has a TTL (Time to Live) value that controls how long resolvers cache the response before requesting a fresh copy. It's a single number, measured in seconds, and it's one of the...

item.title: Practice Backend Engineering Like You Practice DSA
item.description: Practice Backend Engineering Like You Practice DSA Most platforms teach you how to write algorithms. Very few teach you how to make engineering decisions. That's the difference between solving...

item.title: How Paywalls Actually Work: The Engineering Behind Them
item.description: A paywall is one of the more interesting engineering problems on the web, because the publisher has to satisfy two goals that pull in opposite directions. It needs Google to index the article so...

item.title: Beyond Dictation: How to Extract True Conversation Intelligence from Audio in Seconds
item.description: In the modern data ecosystem, audio is everywhere. We record customer support calls, sales syncs, product brainstorming sessions, voice memos, and podcasts. Yet, for many companies, these...

item.title: I Reduced My System Prompt Tokens by 70% Using a Custom Prompt DSL
item.description: What if we've been writing prompts the wrong way? For the last two years, the AI community has focused on writing better prompts. We've all written prompts that look something like this: You are...

item.title: Rails Health Check
item.description: Beyond /up: Production-Grade Health Checks for Rails and Rack Applications Most applications start with a simple question: Is the process alive? Rails answers that with the built-in /up endpoint....

item.title: The AI Code Trust Debate Is Really a Multi-Repo Visibility Problem
item.description: A Hacker News thread posted this week — 'Ask HN: What is your (AI) dev tech stack / workflow?' — surfaced something the industry has been slow to name directly: skepticism around AI-generated code...

item.title: Most Cloud Exit Strategies Start Too Late — Here's the Architecture Reason Why
item.description: Every cloud exit strategy has the same structural problem: by the time the exit decision gets made, the architecture already made it impossible. Not expensive. Not risky. Non-computable. You can't...

item.title: Boox’s new e-reader could replace your Kindle, Kobo, and digital notepad
item.description: Boox has announced a new version of its smallest e-reader that expands the Go 6's functionality to now include note-taking. The new Go 6 (Gen II) sticks with a 6-inch, 300PPI E Ink screen like its...

item.title: ServiceLoader is great — until you want a constructor argument
item.description: I was building a small library that reads accounting files — GnuCash first, other formats maybe later. It's the textbook case for pluggable implementations: an AccBook interface in the API module,...

item.title: AgentGuard: The Foundation Missing from Agentic AI Systems
item.description: This article is a follow-up to "The Blind Spot of Agentic AI Systems". If you haven't read it yet, it explains why this tool exists in the first place. A foundation doesn't define what is built....

item.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
item.description: Anthropic has apologized for stealthily throttling its new AI model, Claude Fable 5, with hidden guardrails that undermine both researchers and rivals using it to develop competing systems. The...

item.title: The library rules (and so do library streaming services)
item.description: Summer is almost here, and that means getting outside, having fun in the sun, and touching grass. But if you need to cool down for a bit, allow me to sing the praises of the library. If your local...

item.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.
item.description: For thirty years, vulnerability management ran on a buffer: the months between when a vulnerability was found and when someone could figure out how to weaponize it. The solution was...

item.title: Enhanced License Plate Tracking
item.description: The surveillance company Leonardo wants more data: A surveillance company plans to add sensors to automatic license plate readers (ALPRs) that would mean the devices, as well as capture the...

item.title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
item.description: Google DeepMind is funding research into the potential dangers of situations where millions of different AI agents interact with each other online. According to Rohin Shah, who directs the...

item.title: iFixit Trump phone teardown confirms it’s an HTC dupe
item.description: After getting its hands on a Trump phone and tearing it apart, iFixit has confirmed what I first reported back in February: the T1 Phone is an almost exact duplicate of the HTC U24 Pro. iFixit...

item.title: YouTube is introducing DMs (again)
item.description: YouTube is reintroducing private messaging after testing new ways for users to share videos and "have conversations about them" last year. In an announcement on its official blog, YouTube says...

item.title: Job titles of the future: Nature’s drug designer
item.description: In 2018, after nearly two decades working in Big Pharma, chemist Tim Cernak was ready to put his skills to a new use. For Merck, he’d developed precision therapies for cancer, HIV, and diabetes...

item.title: Inside soccer’s data renaissance
item.description: Imagine tuning in to the opening kickoff of a World Cup match and seeing a player intentionally send the ball all the way down the pitch and right out of bounds on the opponent’s end. Casual fans...

item.title: Why China is betting on big nuclear reactors
item.description: It’s a tale of two nuclear industries. In China, large reactors are coming together at a stunning pace. The country has nearly doubled its nuclear fleet since 2016, reaching nearly 60 gigawatts of...

item.title: OceanLotus Hits Vietnam Investors With SPECTRALVIPER in FireAnt Attack
item.description: The Vietnam-aligned threat actor known as OceanLotus has been attributed to two distinct campaigns that targeted domestic entities and stock investors with a backdoor known as SPECTRALVIPER. The...

item.title: Ryanair is under investigation over charging parents to sit with their kids
item.description: European economy airline Ryanair is under investigation in the UK for charging parents mandatory fees to sit with their children. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said it was looking...

item.title: Deezer launches an AI music detector for other streaming services
item.description: Deezer will now scan your playlists on other streaming platforms to detect AI-generated music. Deezer was the first of the big streaming services to start labeling AI-generated music. It even...

item.title: GitHub to Disable npm Install Scripts by Default to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: GitHub has announced what it said are "breaking changes" coming to npm version 12, one of which turns off install scripts by default to combat software supply chain threats. The changes aim to...

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: The decision comes as India emerges as the world’s largest GCC market.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: If you doubted his genius, doubt no more.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: The company changed course after researchers spoke out against the policy, which would have covertly limited Claude’s ability to develop competing AI models.

item.title: Bluesky is getting ‘communities’
item.description: Bluesky will be getting "communities," which will function as smaller spaces where you can "go deeper and hang out with people who care about the same stuff" sometime this year, according to head...

item.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
item.description: Discover how astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein’s theory of general relativity.
